economic_question: Does the sign of foreign net demand for U.S. equities in the latest officially released Treasury TIC monthly archive identify a risk-bearing state in which holding SPY improves the return/drawdown tradeoff versus a frozen monthly 50/50 SPY-cash benchmark?

source.strategy_input: latest published monthly TIC net foreign demand for U.S. equities
source.method_limit: archive snapshots preserve revision state by decision but do not reconstruct observations unavailable before the first retained release

accepted_input.delayed_2025_rule: the 2025-09-18 archive is reused for the October and November 2025 execution decisions because no later archive was public by the latter cutoff

signal.decision_cutoff: 20:05 America/New_York on the final accepted XNYS session before execution month
signal.release_selection: latest official TIC monthly archive with public release timestamp no later than the decision cutoff
signal.private_component: npr_history line 8 private domestic equity net
signal.official_component: npr_history line 13 official domestic equity net
signal.cash: foreign_equity_demand less than or equal to zero
signal.sign_interpretation: under both reporting perspectives a positive value denotes foreign net demand for U.S. equities

portfolio.decision_at: 20:05 America/New_York on final accepted XNYS session before state month
portfolio.execution: first accepted XNYS open of state month
portfolio.holding: until next monthly execution open; unchanged state keeps existing exposure

measurement.decision_sizing_price: immediately preceding accepted XNYS raw close
measurement.interval_boundary: pretrade NAV marked at the immediately preceding accepted XNYS raw close
measurement.interval_return: successive pretrade boundary NAV ratio minus one
measurement.terminal_boundary: pretrade NAV at the session before the frozen terminal month execution
measurement.maximum_drawdown_path: initial capital followed by each accepted session opening NAV before the terminal boundary
measurement.cash_distributions: ex-date entitlement and pay-date settlement through the shared US Portfolio
measurement.spy_buy_and_hold: buy once at first validation or secondary execution and do not rebalance

splits.secondary_open_rule: only after all validation gates pass

method_limits: historical inference is descriptive and no confirmatory p-value is assigned
the archived series can contain revisions and is only interpreted as published in each retained archive
the retained release archive is not evidence for observations predating its public release
the sign rule does not distinguish the composition, persistence, or causal origin of foreign equity demand
